description: This chart plots the average size of intragranular κ-carbide precipitates as a function of aging temperature for Fe-30.5Mn-8Al-1C austenitic low-density steel subjected to 6 h of isothermal aging, with data presented as mean ± standard deviation. The x-axis represents aging temperature ranging from 550 °C to 650 °C, while the y-axis measures intragranular κ-carbide size in nanometers. A clear monotonic increasing trend is observed: precipitate size rises from ~11 nm at 550 °C, to ~18 nm at 600 °C, up to ~24 nm at 650 °C, confirming that intragranular κ-carbide undergoes significant coarsening as aging temperature increases for a fixed 6 h aging duration. This quantifies the evolution of κ-carbide precipitation state, which is demonstrated to be a critical factor controlling the tensile and fatigue performance of the tested low-density steel, supporting the core conclusion that adjusting aging temperature tunes precipitate size to optimize mechanical properties.
